title: Ignatius to the Romans VIII
body: I no longer wish to live after the manner of men, and my desire shall be fulfilled if ye consent. Be ye willing, then, that ye also may have your desires fulfilled. I entreat you in this brief letter; do ye give credit to me. Jesus Christ will reveal these things to you, [so that ye shall know] that I speak truly. He is the mouth altogether free from falsehood, by which the Father has truly spoken. Pray ye for me, that I may attain [the object of my desire]. I have not written to you according to the flesh, but according to the will of God. If I shall suffer, ye have wished [well] to me; but if I am rejected, ye have hated me.

source.label: Ignatius to the Romans (shorter recension; trans. Roberts-Donaldson, 1885)

author: engine

witnesses.label: Middle Recension — Codex Mediceus Laurentianus 57.7 (10-11th cent)
Polycarp, Letter to the Philippians — cites Ignatius's letters by name
